title: Stage 09 — Storyboard (typed frames)
description: Typed I/O contract for /mkt-creative-run Stage 09 (design §2/§6.1 — no free-text handoffs). Stage 09 turns the Stage-08 script into a frame-by-frame storyboard and is the bridge to Stage 10 (each frame's image_prompt_seed is what the image stage consumes). This schema formalizes the hand-built CHECKPOINT-1 sample (context/proposed/creative-runs/20260608-072919/sample-stage-09-storyboard.md): each frame names its visual, on-screen text, VO, motion, the purpose/force it serves (purpose_force_ref), the image-prompt seed, and every claim it asserts (claim_refs). Claim/number text is ALWAYS a deterministic caption overlay, never baked into a generated image — so claim_refs travel with the frame, not the image prompt. provenance: run_id + derived_from (the Stage-08 script).

claimRef.description: A reference to one approved/declared claim id in context/claims.json (e.g. "CLM-008"). The bare id only — render markers ([[...]]/{{...}}) are presentation. The deterministic claims floor reads these to fail-close on unverified claims.

claimRefs.description: Every claim id this frame's caption text asserts (empty when the frame makes no PrepEdu claim). Typed surface for the claims gate — never inferred from prose.

forceRef.description: The JTBD purpose/force this frame serves (push / pull / anxiety / habit), per context/proposed/motivator-map.md. A brand/CTA-repeat frame may serve a force or none; see purpose_force_ref.

run_id.description: Timestamped run id (e.g. 20260608-072919) — ties this artifact to the run manifest.

derived_from.description: The Stage-08 script artifact this storyboard realizes (e.g. "sample-stage-08-script.md"). Records the 1:1 beat→frame derivation.

spec.description: Production spec locked for every frame. canvas/duration_s are required; the remaining production directions are optional standing constraints carried from the script.

muted_first.description: Whether the storyboard is muted-first — every line burned in as a caption; VO additive, never required.

frame_count.description: Number of frames (optional convenience; should equal frames.length).

frames.description: The ordered storyboard frames. Each is the typed form of one row in the sample's 'Storyboard frames' table (frame / time / visual / on-screen text / VO / motion / purpose).

frame.description: 1-based frame number (typically 1:1 with the Stage-08 beat seq).

timecode.description: The frame's time window as authored (e.g. "0.0-2.0s" / "9.0–11.5s"). A label, not parsed.

visual.description: The visual / shot description for the frame.

on_screen_text.description: Burned-in on-screen caption (locale-native, muted-first). The surface the deterministic claims floor runs publish-mode against. Empty string when the frame has no caption.

vo.description: Voice-over line (locale-native). Empty string when the frame has no VO (e.g. an end-card that holds, muted-safe).

motion.description: Motion / transition direction for the frame.

image_prompt_seed.description: The seed prompt the Stage-10 image generator would expand for this frame's base image. Claim/number text is NEVER baked here — it is overlaid as a deterministic caption (see claim_refs), so an image model cannot distort it.
